review_title: A good company to start your career
review_body_text: I love this company because of the benefits and good salary. However i am not fit this job because of the position given is not up to my par in which I as a staff know where is my strengths and weaknesses. As personal financial advisor, i have to take care investment and insurance domain in which i know that is not my strength. However i am good in other particular products but i was not given the opportunity to try out other position by the management in which the line manager has tried to propose to the management. However I am very delightful to be given 5his opportunity to work with such a giant company as my first job.

review_title: Not going to work in their sales department
review_body_text: Target set by the manager was beyond reach for rural area. Also, I was smirk at when they ask me my pointer during the interview for the permanent position. How is that professional?
The colleagues are very friendly and helpful. The environment is fun and happening. The manager is kind to the staff and very helpful. They are also easy to approach.
cons_text: stressful and too many work to do everyday

review_body_text: Daily basis, will be given a list of customer to do reminder call and consult for the debt repayment. in charge for credit card due payment for less than six months. In charge for Hight net wealth individual. working culture always play with strategy as to persuade people to pay debt. hardest part when festive season just around the corner well total amount collected is lesser. most enjoyable when hit target early and the list to call is reduce.
location: Akademi Etiqa Masjed Jamek Kuala Lumpur
pros_text: motivated when hit target and earn commission
cons_text: calling people on daily basis, sometimes the same person call over and over gain

review_body_text: Fun workplace with fun colleagues. The staffs and the boss are very friendly. There are many benefits working at Maybank especially the leave and the bonus.

Relaxed workplace where you don't learn much immediately but on the long run can help you understand the whole banking business process. If you are into learning new things fast, then not the right place
Promote financial products to bank customers and must always achieve target given.Must be able yo answer all customers queries and always be prepared to assist any difficulties.

review_title: Good working environment, A lot lessons learned
review_body_text: Been working for 5 days in a week which is on weekday, i have learned a lot of things in banking line since i get started. Very nice workplace, security in salary and future.
cons_text: Hectic at times, long work days

review_body_text: My ideal working environment is one where there's a good sense of team spirit. A strong work ethic is obviously important but the human side is also important. I enjoy working with people who have a decent sense of humour and who, while they might take their work very seriously, don't necessarily take themselves overly seriously! I like people who are down to earth but who have a dynamic and progressive approach to their work. I really enjoy working as part of a highly committed and professional team

Sometimes it was a hustle and bustle of daily office life but it was a great opportunity to learn new skills and knowledge. The hardest part was when I had to attend anger and fussy customer but that built up my skills and confidence level in customer service. The most enjoyable part of the job was when I got rewarded for my worked by the management.

review_title: The best medical benefit among banks
review_body_text: need to be optimistic and willing to bend backwards for all the task given. job is interesting and challenging but work as a great platform for self development and good work attitude ( for fresh graduate). bank core value is held upmost by superior. high integrity is the main focus by all employer.
pros_text: great medical benefit, fixed yearly salary increment
cons_text: long working hours. need to work on weekend and holidays.

review_body_text: Working environment is great, coworkers are helpful and friendly "Friendly culture, helpful seniors, good place to learn" "Long Working Hours and tight deadlines" "Lower-than-average work life balance and lack of transparency for goals and needs"
I have being in Maybank with Friendly Environment and nice colleagues. I have being in Maybank for 7 years with full knowledge and able tomanage all level of people accordingly

review_title: Opportunity to engage with different levels of people
review_body_text: Opportunity to engage with different levels of people, from as low as internal clerical staff to as high as CEO of the companies or people who are classify as Ultra High Networth clients, to provide them with market updates and financial investment opportunnities.
pros_text: Being involved in many Socials Channel example "Maybank Golf Championship Malaysia 2016", Ferrari and Bentley Car Event, and many more to be mentioned
cons_text: Work is beyond the regular standard working hours.

review_body_text: Good place to learn but not as career. Get investment and banking knowledge.More experience on handling customer and more caferul about compliance or Bank Negara Requirements.
Develope skil in handling people. Able to emphathy and giving solution to customer inquiry and request. I enjoy working here eventhough im in shift but i get allowance and travel allowance.

review_title: I enjoy and have fun in working place
review_body_text: I enjoy the work.Sometimes feel stress.I learn how to work in groups.The management ok wit me.I enjoy benefits like medical and holiday offer. I learn the time value.
My day to day job is busy as well as challenging in meeting the time and SLA requirement of the organisation. I enjoy meeting with meeting and solving them with their issues daily.
Guide customer to apply loan Always share market info with colleagues Customer will appreciate my hard work when getting their home loan A healthy working environment
It was a good fun place. Malaysia's biggest bank gave huge opportunities to learn. Work culture was a bit autocratic but they wanted fresh graduates to learn.
